headline: Lambda Calculus
description: Lambda calculus (Alonzo Church, 1936) is a formal system for expressing computation via function abstraction and application. It is Turing-complete and the theoretical foundation of functional programming (Lisp, Haskell, ML, Clojure, JavaScript's arrow functions).

citation.name: Types and Programming Languages (2025 Updated Edition)
citation.author: Pierce B.C.

citation.name: The Lambda Calculus: Its Syntax and Semantics (2025 Revised)
citation.author: Barendregt H.
